pattern: Parsing the real and imaginary integers and applying the i-squared rule produces the product string in canonical form.
hints: Split each string at the plus that precedes the imaginary part and drop the trailing i.
Multiply like binomials, remembering that the product of the two imaginary parts contributes negatively to the real part.
Real result is a*c minus b*d and imaginary result is a*d plus b*c; format as real+imaginaryi.

edge_inputs.description: i times i gives negative real

stress.description: random integer real and imaginary parts in range
